edge_hypothesis: RSI pullback is the system's 3rd viable template (PF=1.21, 38 trades, FRAGILE ACCEPT at 4:1 R:R). The signal logic — price above EMA200 + positive EMA50 slope + RSI dipping to 35-40 — identifies genuine trend pullbacks. This is fundamentally a 'buy the dip in an uptrend' strategy. The advisory identified two critical facts: (1) RSI<30 produces 0 trades — too restrictive, keep RSI≤40 minimum; (2) extreme R:R is the universal PF amplifier. The pullback signal is uniquely suited to tail harvesting because the setup itself describes the beginning of a continuation move: price dips briefly against a strong trend (RSI falls to 35-40), then the trend resumes. These continuation moves frequently produce 5-10+ ATR extensions as the prior trend reasserts. At 4:1 R:R, we're clipping these continuations early. At 7-8:1, we capture the full trend leg. The key risk is trade count: on 4h with RSI≤40 we got only 38 trades. On 1h we should get 80-150 trades, clearing the 30-trade gate with margin. Also testing RSI≤45 on 4h to increase entry frequency — slightly less oversold but still a meaningful pullback from trend. Regime: trending-dominant (by construction — requires price>EMA200+slope). Invalidation: if PF decreases from 4x to 7x TP, RSI pullback signals don't produce fat-tail continuations — the dip-buy catches range-bound oscillations, not trend continuations.

variants.name: rsi_pullback_1h_tail_harvest_7to1
variants.description: RSI pullback with EMA200 trend + EMA50 slope on ETH/1h with 7:1 R:R. The primary variant. RSI≤40 (proven to fire on 4h, should produce 80-150 entries on 1h). The pullback-into-uptrend signal catches the moment when a brief correction ends and the prior trend resumes. At 7:1 R:R (2.0x stop, 14.0x TP), we need ~15% of these continuation moves to reach 14x ATR. In a strong uptrend after a pullback to RSI=38, a 14x ATR rally is a normal trend leg. Win rate will be lower than 4:1 but PF should be higher due to the extreme R:R payoff asymmetry.
entry_long: Close > EMA_200 (price is in an uptrend).
EMA_50 slope is positive (EMA_50 rising bar-over-bar).
RSI_14 <= 40 (pullback detected — price has dipped within the uptrend).
entry_short: Close < EMA_200 (price is in a downtrend).
EMA_50 slope is negative (EMA_50 falling bar-over-bar).
RSI_14 >= 60 (rally detected — price has bounced within the downtrend).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (14.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < PF at 4x (1.21), pullback signals don't produce tail continuations — stick with 4:1 R:R
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 14.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_200 trend direction + EMA_50 slope confirmation — ensures we only buy dips in confirmed uptrends
RSI_14 <= 40 — pullback detection, not deep oversold (RSI<30 = 0 trades)
ATR_14 for 2.0x stop / 14.0x TP — 7:1 R:R tail harvesting on trend continuations

variants.name: rsi_pullback_4h_relaxed_entry_8to1
variants.description: RSI pullback with EMA200 trend + EMA50 slope on ETH/4h with RSI≤45 (relaxed from default 40) and 8:1 R:R. The 4h variant with relaxed entry threshold. RSI≤40 produced only 38 trades on 4h — barely above gate. RSI≤45 captures shallower pullbacks that are still meaningful dips (RSI 41-45 = price pulled back but not deeply). With 8:1 R:R (2.0x stop, 16.0x TP), the shallower entry should be offset by extreme payoff. This tests whether RSI pullback signal quality degrades at RSI≤45 or whether the shallower pullbacks produce comparable continuation moves.
entry_long: Close > EMA_200 (price is in an uptrend).
EMA_50 slope is positive (EMA_50 rising bar-over-bar).
RSI_14 <= 45 (relaxed pullback — shallow dip within uptrend).
entry_short: Close < EMA_200 (price is in a downtrend).
EMA_50 slope is negative (EMA_50 falling bar-over-bar).
RSI_14 >= 55 (relaxed inverse — shallow bounce within downtrend).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (16.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < 1.0 with RSI≤45, shallow pullbacks don't produce quality continuations — keep RSI≤40
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 16.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_200 trend + EMA_50 slope — uptrend confirmation
RSI_14 <= 45 — RELAXED from 40, testing whether shallow pullbacks produce quality continuations
ATR_14 for 2.0x stop / 16.0x TP — 8:1 R:R compensates for potentially lower signal quality at RSI≤45

variants.name: rsi_pullback_1h_moderate_rr_5to1
variants.description: RSI pullback with EMA200 trend + EMA50 slope on ETH/1h with 5:1 R:R. The control variant — moderate R:R on 1h. Tests whether the RSI pullback on 1h produces enough trades (expected 80-150) with a moderate R:R that balances win rate and payoff. 5:1 is between the proven 4:1 (PF=1.21 on 4h) and extreme 7:1. If PF improves from 4:1 to 5:1, the tail harvesting thesis holds for RSI pullback but with a flatter curve than MACD. 1.5x ATR stop (minimum floor) with 7.5x ATR TP.
entry_long: Close > EMA_200 (price is in an uptrend).
EMA_50 slope is positive (EMA_50 rising bar-over-bar).
RSI_14 <= 40 (pullback detected — standard threshold, proven to produce trades).
entry_short: Close < EMA_200 (price is in a downtrend).
EMA_50 slope is negative (EMA_50 falling bar-over-bar).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (7.5x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < 1.21 (4h baseline), 1h timeframe degrades RSI pullback signal quality
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 7.5x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_200 trend + EMA_50 slope — standard uptrend confirmation
RSI_14 <= 40 — standard pullback threshold, proven to fire
ATR_14 for 1.5x stop / 7.5x TP — 5:1 R:R control variant between proven 4:1 and extreme 7:1
